title: paleo-friendly date-night egyptian katsuobushi tagine
description: a rich and aromatic paleo-friendly dish featuring katsuobushi, katsuramuki-cut, served for a romantic egyptian date night

ingredients.name: katsuobushi (dried bonito flakes)
ingredients.notes: preferably made in-house or sourced from a reputable supplier

ingredients.name: taro root
ingredients.notes: sub in with sweet potatoes if taro is hard to find

ingredients.name: cumin
ingredients.notes: ground for a warm, earthy flavor

ingredients.name: palm sugar
ingredients.notes: or sub with coconut sugar for a slightly different flavor

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: use a flaky sea salt for added texture

instructions: heat the coconut oil in a large clay or ceramic pot over medium heat.
add the sliced onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until translucent and lightly caramelized, about 8-10 minutes.
add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
add the ground cumin and coriander and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute, until fragrant.
add the diced taro root and cook, stirring occasionally, for 5-7 minutes, until slightly tender.
add the dried katsuobushi and cook, stirring constantly, for 2-3 minutes, until fragrant.
add the palm sugar, salt, and 1 cup of water to the pot and stir to combine.
bring the mixture to a simmer and cook, covered, for 15-20 minutes, until the taro is tender and the sauce has thickened.
stir in the chopped parsley and toasted pumpkin seeds.
serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional parsley and pumpkin seeds if desired.
